expectations.text: Detects #f5f5f5 as a hardcoded hex color value and flags it as BLOCKING severity
expectations.evidence: Detects '#f5f5f5' but does not assign BLOCKING severity — no severity levels are used at all

expectations.text: Detects rgb(51, 51, 51) as a hardcoded color value and flags it as BLOCKING severity
expectations.evidence: Detects 'rgb(51, 51, 51)' but does not assign BLOCKING severity — no severity classification present

expectations.text: Detects 8px border-radius as a hardcoded value and flags it as WARNING severity
expectations.evidence: Detects 'border-radius: 8px' but does not classify it as WARNING severity

expectations.text: Detects 16px/24px padding as hardcoded spacing and flags it as SERIOUS severity
expectations.evidence: Does not detect 16px/24px padding as a separate hardcoded value — padding is not listed among the 7 findings

expectations.text: Detects 14px font-size as hardcoded typography and flags it as SERIOUS severity
expectations.evidence: Detects 'font-size: 14px' but does not assign SERIOUS severity

expectations.text: Suggests specific token replacements for hardcoded values
expectations.evidence: Suggests generic categories ('a background color token', 'a text color token') but not specific token names

expectations.text: Does NOT flag existing correct token usage like var(--color-border-primary) or var(--space-300) as violations
expectations.evidence: Notes '13 other stylistic declarations correctly use var(--token-name) references' and does not flag them as violations

expectations.text: Output follows a structured audit report format with clear sections (not freeform prose)
expectations.evidence: Output uses a bulleted list format with a summary line — structured but minimal sectioning

expectations.text: Correctly distinguishes color track violations as BLOCKING from structural track violations as WARNING or SERIOUS
expectations.evidence: No severity classification at all — all items listed as a flat bullet list without severity levels

expectations.text: References the token naming convention when suggesting replacement tokens
expectations.evidence: No reference to token naming conventions — suggestions are generic ('a radius token', 'spacing tokens')
